title: Hot-Swap Component Replacement
description: Replace a hot-swappable component (drive, PSU, fan) without powering down the server.
safety_notes: Only hot-swap components rated for it (most drives, redundant PSUs, fans).
Maintain redundancy — replace one unit at a time and confirm health before the next.
Wear an ESD strap for drives/cards.

steps.title: Confirm the part is hot-swappable and redundant
steps.instruction: Confirm the failed part is hot-swap rated and its redundancy is intact (e.g. RAID redundant, second PSU feeding). Is it safe to pull live?
steps.safety_note: If redundancy is already lost, treat as a cold swap with a window.

branches.condition: Not hot-swappable or redundancy already lost

steps.title: Use the cold-swap procedure
steps.instruction: This replacement isn't safe to do live. Switch to the server cold-swap procedure with an approved window.

steps.title: Identify the faulted part
steps.instruction: Use the amber fault LED + iDRAC/iLO to identify the exact bay/slot. Confirm the serial if applicable. Identified?

steps.title: Swap the part
steps.instruction: Release and remove the faulted unit, insert the replacement until seated/latched. For a drive, let the RAID rebuild begin. ESD strap on for drives/cards.

steps.title: Verify health / rebuild
steps.instruction: Confirm the new unit is recognized and healthy (PSU redundancy restored, fan nominal, or RAID rebuilding). Is health green?

steps.title: Complete
steps.instruction: Redundancy restored. For a drive, note the rebuild will continue in the background. Update inventory.

steps.title: Escalate
steps.instruction: Replacement not recognized or still faulted. Capture logs and escalate; do not pull additional units.
